title: calophyllum rivulare
intro: Calophyllum rivulare, a species of plant in the family Clusiaceae, is commonly known as the red beech or red mahogany. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it a popular choice for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Calophyllum rivulare include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Calophyllum rivulare is often prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
